title: Authors and Citation
text: Athanasia Mo Mowinckel. Author, maintainer. Didac Vidal-Piñeiro. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mowinckel , Vidal-Piñeiro D (2026). ggsegGlasser: Glasser Atlas 'ggsegverse' Ecosystem. R package version 2.0.3, https://github.com/ggsegverse/ggsegGlasser.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggsegGlasser: Glasser Atlas for the 'ggsegverse' Ecosystem},   author = {Athanasia Mo Mowinckel and Didac Vidal-Piñeiro},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2.0.3},   url = {https://github.com/ggsegverse/ggsegGlasser}, }

what: Glasser Atlas for the ggsegverse Ecosystem
title: Glasser Atlas for the ggsegverse Ecosystem
text: repository contains R package atlas data ggseg ggseg3d Glasser parcellation HPC. Glasser et al. (2016) Nature, volume 536, pages 171-178 pubmed learn use atlases, please look documentation ggseg ggseg3d. recommend installing ggseg-atlases ggseg r-universe: development version GitHub :   HCP-MMP1 annotation files (fsaverage space, resampled fsaverage5). Reference: Glasser et al. (2016) doi:10.1038/nature18933
code: options(   repos = c(     ggseg = "https://ggseg.r-universe.dev",     CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ) )  install.packages("ggsegGlasser") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ggseg/ggsegGlasser") library(ggseg) library(ggsegGlasser) library(ggplot2)  ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = glasser(),     mapping = aes(fill = label),     position = position_brain(hemi ~ view),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   scale_fill_manual(values = glasser()$palette, na.value = "grey") +   theme_void() library(ggseg3d)  ggseg3d(atlas = glasser()) |>   pan_camera("right lateral")

title: Glasser Atlas for the ggsegverse Ecosystem
text: recommend installing ggseg-atlases ggseg r-universe: development version GitHub :
code: options(   repos = c(     ggseg = "https://ggseg.r-universe.dev",     CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ) )  install.packages("ggsegGlasser") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ggseg/ggsegGlasser")

title: Glasser Atlas for the ggsegverse Ecosystem
code: library(ggseg) library(ggsegGlasser) library(ggplot2)  ggplot() +   geom_brain(     atlas = glasser(),     mapping = aes(fill = label),     position = position_brain(hemi ~ view),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   scale_fill_manual(values = glasser()$palette, na.value = "grey") +   theme_void() library(ggseg3d)  ggseg3d(atlas = glasser()) |>   pan_camera("right lateral")

title: Glasser Atlas for the ggsegverse Ecosystem
text: HCP-MMP1 annotation files (fsaverage space, resampled fsaverage5). Reference: Glasser et al. (2016) doi:10.1038/nature18933

what: ggsegGlasser: Glasser Atlas for the 'ggsegverse' Ecosystem — ggsegGlasser-package
title: ggsegGlasser: Glasser Atlas for the 'ggsegverse' Ecosystem — ggsegGlasser-package
text: Glasser multi-modal cortical parcellation (HCP) atlas 'ggseg' ecosystem. Provides unified 'ggseg_atlas' object 2D polygon geometry use 'ggseg'.

title: ggsegGlasser: Glasser Atlas for the 'ggsegverse' Ecosystem — ggsegGlasser-package
text: Maintainer: Athanasia Mo Mowinckel .m.mowinckel@psykologi.uio.(ORCID) Authors: Didac Vidal-Piñeiro d.v.pineiro@psykologi.uio.(ORCID)

title: Glasser Multi-Modal Cortical Parcellation (HCP) — glasser
text: Brain atlas Glasser cortical parcellation 361 regions based Human Connectome Project multi-modal parcellation. Contains 2D polygon geometry ggseg::geom_brain().

title: Glasser Multi-Modal Cortical Parcellation (HCP) — glasser
text: Glasser MF, Coalson TS, Robinson EC, Hacker CD, Harwell J, Yacoub E, Ugurbil K, Andersson J, Beckmann CF, Jenkinson M, Smith SM, Van Essen DC (2016). multi-modal parcellation human cerebral cortex. Nature, 536(7615):171-178. doi:10.1038/nature18933

title: Glasser Multi-Modal Cortical Parcellation (HCP) — glasser
code: glasser() #>  #> ── glasser ggseg atlas ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: cortical #> Regions: 180 #> Hemispheres: left, right #> Views: inferior, lateral, medial, superior #> Palette: ✔ #> Rendering: ✔ ggseg #> ✔ ggseg3d (vertices) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> # A tibble: 360 × 3 #>     hemi
